question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's goroutine scheduler to be starved (e.g., via CPU exhaustion from a co-located workload) prevent the heartbeat goroutine from writing /tmp/alive within the 15-second window, causing the liveness probe to fail and masking a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing the address of a Tor exit node or anonymizing proxy, causing the pinger to route consensus traffic through attacker-controlled infrastructure and inducing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '1ns', causing all HTTP requests in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to immediately timeout, preventing the pinger from ever building a valid network map and inducing a startup-time network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing the address of a DNS sinkhole, causing the pinger to resolve node hostnames to non-routable IPs and inducing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the Hiero network to return NODE_IS_FROZEN status on every transaction cause submitWithRetry() to exhaust all retries without recovery, creating an effective network partition that persists for the duration of the freeze?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's network namespace to be isolated (e.g., via a Kubernetes NetworkPolicy change) prevent all outbound connections from the pinger, causing submitWithRetry() to fail on every attempt and creating a network partition that is not self-healing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker cause the mirror node REST endpoint to return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing the address of a load balancer that routes to a subset of consensus nodes, causing the pinger to only communicate with a partition of the network and miss failures on the excluded nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the Hiero SDK's connection pool to be exhausted (e.g., by opening many concurrent connections to consensus nodes) cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to block waiting for a connection, consuming the retry budget and creating a sustained network partition?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who can cause the pinger's container to be OOM-killed (e.g., via memory exhaustion from a co-located workload) terminate the pinger process, causing a gap in network partition monitoring that allows a real partition to go undetected?
